title: 5 Markers of God’s Voice
hook: Unsure if it’s God or your mind? Use these 5 markers.
script: Unsure if it’s God or your mind? Use these 5 markers. God’s voice matches Scripture—He won’t contradict His Word. It carries peace, not panic, even when it challenges you. It’s confirmed in community through wise, godly counsel. It bears good fruit over time—more love, joy, and self-control, not confusion. And timing lines up—doors open without manipulation. Example: “Quit today out of fear” screams rush; “Prepare, apply, trust Me” aligns with peace, process, and counsel. Quick checklist: Pray, open the Word, note your peace level, ask one mature believer, sleep on it. If it still stands, step in faith.

title: The 60-Second God-Voice Test
hook: You may be overthinking this—here’s a 60-second God-voice test.
script: You may be overthinking this—here’s a 60-second God-voice test. Breathe and pray, “Jesus, lead me.” Open one verse that speaks to the decision; God won’t guide against His Word. Scan your body—peace calms, panic pushes. Message a mature believer and invite honest feedback. Imagine obeying a week from now—would it grow love, joy, and self-control, or anxiety and shame? Finally, check timing: are doors opening without you forcing them? Summary to replay: Word, Peace, Counsel, Fruit, Timing. If four out of five say yes, move gently. If fear is driving, slow down and wait.

title: When God Feels Silent: Discernment Keys
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. His voice is usually steady, not shouty. Look for five markers. It agrees with Scripture—He won’t tell you to compromise. It brings settled courage, not frantic urgency. It’s echoed by wise community. It produces good fruit—more patience, kindness, and clarity. And the timing isn’t forced; God opens doors. Picture this: “Defend yourself now!” feels hot and hurried. “Speak the truth with gentleness tomorrow” aligns with the Word, brings peace, your mentor nods, the fruit is reconciliation, and the timing allows prayer. Two-minute practice: read a Psalm, breathe, ask counsel, sleep on it, then obey the clearest light.
